text.title: Team Development Worked Examples
text.summary: Two worked examples: navigating Storming-stage conflict and first-hire integration for a solo-to-team transition.

example.note: Situation: two developers in architectural disagreement that is slowing the team and heating meetings.
example.code:
Symptoms observed: - Developer A and Developer B argue about microservice vs. monolith in every planning meeting - Retrospective atmosphere is tense; others are silent - Sprint velocity dropped 30% over 3 sprints - Both devs are updating architecture docs independently
PM actions (in order): 1. Meet individually with A and B — understand each person's technical concern and personal stake 2. Facilitate a structured session with explicit ground rules: one speaker, no interruptions, timer 3. Frame the discussion as "what does the system need?" not "who is right?" 4. Document the agreed-upon architectural decision as an ADR within 24 hours 5. Share ADR with full team; name the decision-maker if no consensus
Week 3 follow-up: - Both devs cite ADR in code reviews instead of re-litigating - Retro tone returns to constructive - Velocity recovers to baseline

example.note: Situation: solo founder hiring first team member; no existing process documentation.
example.code:
Before hire arrives: - Write down every implicit process (deployment, code review, naming conventions) - Create a "how we work" document (2 pages max) - Set up repo access, Slack, 1password, and dev environment guide
Week 1: Orientation - Walk through codebase together; explain architectural decisions - Shadow founder for 2 days: observe how decisions get made - Define initial responsibilities in writing
Weeks 2-4: Gradual Ownership - Pair on first 3 features; founder drives, new hire navigates - Swap: new hire drives, founder reviews + explains - Give feedback within 24 hours of PR submission - Weekly 1-on-1: what's unclear? what would help?
Month 2: Independence - Assign a feature with clear acceptance criteria; no pairing - Check in at midpoint, not daily - Ask new hire to document one process they think is unclear - Celebrate first solo ship in team channel
Key: document everything before hire arrives; verbal-only onboarding creates dependency on the founder and slows the transition to independence.

section.title: Skills Matrix Update from Git Data

example.note: Use git history to provide evidence-backed initial scores for a skills matrix.
example.code: # Infer expertise per directory from git contribution history (last 180 days) since="180 days ago" for dir in $(git ls-tree -d --name-only HEAD); do echo "## $dir" git log --since="$since" --pretty=format:'%an' -- "$dir" \ | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5 echo done
example.note: Use output as starting evidence; do not auto-populate scores without human review.

text.reference: Team charter template for the onboarding examples above
Skills matrix with 1-4 scale and gap-action column
